meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no version control (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no version control. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing & Version Control for Students: Expert Guide
content.seo_description: Master SaaS pricing and fix version control issues. A hands-on, intermediate tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code to build your first professional app.
content.answer_block: Version control is the bedrock of professional development; without it, your SaaS pricing logic is fragile and risky. By integrating Git-based versioning with Claude Code and Antigravity, you secure your codebase, automate pricing deployments, and build the professional infrastructure required to launch a production-ready SaaS application.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks crafting the perfect logic for your SaaS pricing model, only to realize that a single accidental keystroke or failed deployment could wipe out your progress. The 'no version control' trap is the silent killer of student projects, transforming your promising startup into a graveyard of lost files and fragmented code.

But today, we stop hacking and start engineering. By architecting your pricing engine with robust version control and AI-assisted deployment, you move from a student tinkering with scripts to a developer shipping scalable software. We are going to lock your progress, refine your pricing logic, and deploy with the confidence of a seasoned architect.

steps.title: Initialize Git-Backed Architecture
steps.description: Establish a secure foundation for your codebase to prevent data loss. This ensures every pricing iteration is tracked.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new repository structure for a SaaS application with clean separation between the pricing engine and user authentication modules.
steps.claude_code_command: git init && git add . && git commit -m 'Initial secure commit for SaaS pricing engine'

steps.title: Define Tiered Pricing Logic
steps.description: Create a modular pricing model that adapts to user growth, making your code easier to maintain and version.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a clean, modular pricing service that calculates monthly recurring revenue based on three distinct tiers: Basic, Pro, and Enterprise.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add pricing_service.py --context 'ensure modularity for future tier additions'

steps.title: Implement Version-Controlled Webhooks
steps.description: Ensure your payment webhooks are versioned to handle API updates from providers like Stripe without breaking your app.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a webhook handler that validates incoming payment signals and logs them into a version-controlled event store.

steps.title: Automate Deployment Pipeline
steps.description: Connect your repository to an automated deployment flow, ensuring only tested code hits production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment script that validates unit tests before pushing updates to the production environment.

steps.title: Audit and Tag Release v1.0
steps.description: Tag your stable build to create a permanent snapshot of your working pricing logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a security audit on the current pricing logic and generate a summary for documentation.
steps.claude_code_command: git tag -a v1.0 -m 'Stable pricing engine release'

pro_tips: Always use semantic versioning (SemVer) for your pricing APIs to prevent breaking changes for existing users.
Implement environment variables for your Stripe API keys; never hardcode credentials in your repository.
Use Git branches to experiment with new pricing tiers without affecting your live production environment.
Create a 'mock' pricing service for unit testing to verify logic without triggering real financial transactions.
Commit your documentation alongside your code to ensure your future self understands the 'why' behind complex pricing logic.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing engine is version-controlled, focus on integrating a frontend dashboard to visualize your MRR and user acquisition metrics.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just writing code; you are building a scalable business asset. By mastering version control and professional pricing architecture, you've leveled up your engineering career. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
